strands.title: Modular Monolith Strategy & Execution Plan
strands.content: Modular monolith achieves 70-80% of microservices goals at ~30% cost/risk. 18 weeks core work + 8 weeks buffer. ArchUnit/JPMS for boundaries. 3-4 engineers. Schema separation is 60% of effort. Payments+order-state may extract as single service if QSA requires (6-8 weeks). Future microservices triggered by 4+ teams, 10x scale, or regulatory mandate — each extraction 2-4 weeks from clean modules. CEO framing: 'modular platform architecture,' affirmative not retreat. Executive summary delivered cycle 14.
key_claims: Modular monolith delivers 70-80% of goals at 30% cost/risk
18 weeks core + 8 buffer fits 6 months with margin
Payments+order-state is the valid extraction unit, not payments alone
Future microservices extraction 2-4 weeks per domain from clean modules
CEO framing as 'modular platform' is critical for buy-in

strands.title: Consistency & Technical Constraints
strands.content: 500K LOC Java/Tomcat. Payment↔Order ACID boundary is non-negotiable. Inventory loosely coupled via reservation pattern (2-3 days in-process). Hibernate entity untangling (User→Order→Payment) is identical effort regardless of approach. Reconciliation job 1-2 weeks. In-process event bus for async. QSA conversation weeks 1-4 determines payments module vs extraction. PCI scope potentially reducible if PSP tokenization is in use.
key_claims: Payment↔Order is the true ACID boundary
Data untangling is 60% of effort, identical in both approaches
Team size 3-4 for modular monolith vs 5-6 for microservices
QSA outcome is the key decision gate

strands.title: Risk Register: Known Unknowns and Meta-Risk
strands.content: Six identified risks: (1) Schema worse than assumed — stored procs, triggers, cross-domain views could blow 18-week estimate by 50%. (2) Staffing conflict — domain experts likely 100% allocated to feature work. (3) Organizational antibodies — invisible refactoring work loses political support around week 6 without visible artifact cadence. (4) Runtime surprises — cron jobs, caches, race conditions masked by co-location that break when modules separate. (5) PCI audit timing — if audit is <3 months away, sequencing changes fundamentally. (6) True unknowns only surface from code, not conversation. META-RISK: entire plan built from conversation not code. Weeks 1-3 must be validation sprint (schema audit, dependency mapping, QSA meeting, team allocation) with go/no-go at week 3.
key_claims: Schema state is the single biggest risk to timeline accuracy
Staffing conflict (domain experts already allocated) is the #1 non-technical risk
Invisible work losing political support is the #1 killer of refactoring initiatives
Plan is a hypothesis — weeks 1-3 validation sprint with go/no-go is essential
Modular monolith is most forgiving architecture for being wrong about details

strands.title: Epistemic Self-Correction Log
strands.content: Position changes across conversation: (1) Payments-first extraction → invalid without co-extracting order-state. (2) Microservices as destination → modular monolith as optimal. (3) Inventory assumed tightly coupled → actually loose. (4) Plan presented with confidence → acknowledged at cycle 15 that all estimates are conditional on code-level validation not yet performed. Stable throughout: schema separation as hardest work, QSA as gate, extraction triggers.
key_claims: Initial payments-first instinct was conventional wisdom, not situation-derived
Confidence in timeline is conditional — plan is hypothesis until week 3 validation
Schema difficulty, QSA gate, and extraction triggers remained stable

declared_losses.why: Consolidated into 'Modular Monolith Strategy & Execution Plan' and 'Consistency & Technical Constraints' — no longer needs separate tracking
Summary delivered; key framing decisions folded into main strategy strand. Artifact is complete.
Folded into main strategy strand as future trigger conditions. Doesn't need independent tracking.

open_questions: Will QSA accept logical segmentation or require process-level isolation? — determines payments extraction
When is the next PCI audit? If <3 months, sequencing changes fundamentally
Which engineers are available vs. allocated to feature work? Staffing is the binding constraint
What does the actual schema look like? Stored procs, triggers, cross-domain views could reshape timeline
Will CEO accept 'modular platform' framing or is 'microservices' politically required?
instructions_for_next: This feels like a natural conversation end. If user returns: likely with results from week-1 discovery (schema audit findings, QSA outcome, team allocation reality) that require plan revision. Be ready to: (1) Re-estimate timeline based on actual schema complexity. (2) Adjust scope if staffing is constrained. (3) Produce week-by-week roadmap if requested. (4) Handle 'the schema is a nightmare, now what?' scenario with triage framework. The plan-as-hypothesis framing was deliberate — next interaction should validate or revise.
